mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly supporting higher S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions and rising consumer spending power, generating positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment is above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term marginal predictive power is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of contraction months, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation without corresponding monetary tightening signals impending policy normalization, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% MoM while FEDFUNDS remains flat
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, supporting sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows, or payroll growth fails to correlate with quarterly equity returns (Spearman rho < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with accelerating real auto sales confirms broad-based demand momentum, generating positive equity returns over the next month as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is subsumed by monetary policy variables in multivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in industrial production signals immediate manufacturing weakness and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to predict direction with statistical significance (binomial test p > 0.10).
